things.name: Piazza Prampolini (Piazza del Duomo)
things.description: Reggio Emilia's magnificent main square flanked by the Gothic Cathedral, the Bishop's Palace and the Renaissance Town Hall; a perfectly composed civic ensemble

things.name: Musei Civici
things.description: Outstanding civic museums in the historic Palazzo dei Musei with collections of geology, natural history and an important gallery of Emilian Baroque paintings

things.name: Tricolour Museum
things.description: Museum in the Palazzo del Capitano del Popolo where the Italian Tricolour flag was born in 1797; charts the history of Italian national identity and the Risorgimento

things.name: Calatrava Bridges
things.description: Three striking bridges designed by Santiago Calatrava for the new high-speed rail station district; a remarkable contemporary architectural addition to the city

things.name: Reggio Children (Loris Malaguzzi Centre)
things.description: International education centre celebrating the world-famous Reggio Emilia Approach to early childhood education, visited by educators from around the world

things.name: Parmigiano-Reggiano Dairy Visit
things.description: Visit a working dairy producing the king of cheeses, Parmigiano-Reggiano DOP; Reggio Emilia is the co-producer with Parma of the world's greatest hard cheese

things.name: Teatro Romolo Valli
things.description: Neoclassical opera house from 1857 with an ornate interior; one of Emilia Romagna's leading performance venues hosting opera, dance and drama

things.name: Piazza della Vittoria
things.description: Elegant square with the Prampolini monument and surrounded by Liberty-style early 20th-century buildings; a less-visited complement to the Cathedral Square

things.name: Bismantova Rock
things.description: Dante described this extraordinary sandstone mesa in his Purgatorio; now a hiking and climbing destination in the Apennine foothills south of Reggio

things.name: Mapei Stadium - Citta del Tricolore
things.description: Home of US Sassuolo, who played in the UEFA Europa League 2016-17; a modern 21,525-seat stadium shared with Reggiana and used for Italian national team games
